title: Controlling devices in operations areas based on environmental states detected using machine learning
abstract: In some embodiments, a system is provided that includes an edge computing device and at least one camera configured to obtain image data depicting at least a portion of an operations area. The edge computing device includes a non-transitory computer-readable medium that has a model data store and computer-executable instructions stored thereon. The instructions cause the edge computing device to perform actions including receiving at least one image from the at least one camera; processing the at least one image using at least one machine learning model stored in the model data store to determine at least one environmental state within the operations area; and controlling a device based on the determined at least one environmental state. The machine learning model is trained by a model management computing system that obtains training data via low-bandwidth connections to edge computing devices.
claims: Claims CLAIMS The embodiments of the invention in which an exclusive property or privilege is claimed are defined as follows: 1. A method of controlling a device in an operations area, the method comprising: receiving, by an edge computing device from a model management computing system, at least one machine learning model; storing, by the edge computing device, the at least one machine learning model in a model data store of the edge computing device; receiving, by the edge computing device, at least one image from at least one camera configured to obtain image data depicting at least a portion of the operations area; processing, by the edge computing device, the at least one image using the at least one machine learning model to determine at least one environmental state within the operations area; and controlling, by the edge computing device, the device based on the determined at least one environmental state. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein determining the at least one environmental state includes determining whether one or more of a subject apparatus and one or more servicing apparatuses are present in the operations area. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein determining the at least one environmental state includes determining a state of at least one of the subject apparatus and the one or more servicing apparatuses. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein the subject apparatus is an aircraft. 5. The method of claim 4, wherein the one or more servicing apparatuses includes an auto level wheel of a passenger boarding bridge. 33 6. The method of claim 5, wherein determining the state of at least one of the subject apparatus and the one or more servicing apparatuses includes: determining whether a door of the aircraft is open; and determining whether the auto level wheel is extended. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein controlling the device based on the determined at least one environmental state includes, in response to determining that the aircraft is present, the door is open, and the auto level wheel is not engaged, controlling the device to present an alert. 8. The method of claim 7, wherein the device includes a loudspeaker, and wherein controlling the device to present the alert includes controlling the device to present the alert via the loudspeaker. 9. The method of claim 7 or 8, wherein the device includes an Aircraft Communications Addressing and Reporting System (ACARS) transmitter, and wherein controlling the device to present the alert includes controlling the device to transmit the alert via the ACARS transmitter. 10. A system, comprising: at least one camera configured to obtain image data depicting at least a portion of an operations area; and an edge computing device having at least one processor and a non-transitory computer-readable medium; wherein the edge computing device is communicatively coupled to the at least one camera; wherein the non-transitory computer-readable medium has a model data store and computer-executable instructions stored thereon; and wherein the instructions, in response to execution by the at least one processor, cause the edge computing device to perform actions of a method as recited in any one of claims 1 to 9. 34 11. A non-transitory computer-readable medium having computer-executable instructions stored thereon that, in response to execution by one or more processors of an edge computing device, cause the edge computing device to perform actions of a method as recited in any one of claims 1 to 9. 12. An edge computing device configured to perform actions of a method as recited in any one of claims 1 to 9. 13. A method of collecting training data and training one or more machine learning models for use on edge computing devices, the method comprising: receiving, by a model management computing system from one or more edge computing devices via low-bandwidth connections, combined data streams including one or more images; processing, by the model management computing system, the combined data streams to obtain the one or more images; for each image of the one or more images: receiving, by the model management computing system, one or more annotations of one or more objects within the image; and adding, by the model management computing system, the one or more annotations and the image to a training data set; training, by the model management computing system, one or more machine learning models using the training data set to generate new annotations of new images; and transmitting, by the model management computing system, the trained one or more machine learning models to one or more edge computing devices. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein the one or more annotations indicate at least one of a presence of a piece of equipment and a status of a piece of equipment. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein the piece of equipment is an aircraft, an aircraft door, an auto level wheel, or a de-icing machine. 16. The method of any one of claims 13 to 15, wherein processing the combined data streams to obtain the one or more images includes, for a given combined data stream: extracting a set of low-bandwidth video streams in a bandwidth-capped codec from the given combined data stream; transcoding the low-bandwidth video streams from the bandwidth-capped codec to a variable-bandwidth codec to create a set of recovered video streams; and extracting the one or more images from the set of recovered video streams. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein processing the combined data streams to obtain the one or more images further includes: segmenting at least one predetermined portion of at least one of the one or more images to obtain at least one segmented image; and providing the at least one segmented image for inclusion in the one or more images. 18. The method of claim 17, wherein receiving one or more annotations of one or more objects within the image includes, for each predetermined portion and each segmented image associated with the predetermined portion, receiving one or more segment annotations of one or more objects within the segmented image. 19. The method of claim 18, further comprising adding the one or more segment annotations and the segmented image associated with the predetermined portion to a segment training data set associated with the predetermined portion. 20. The method of claim 19, further comprising training a segment machine learning model for each predetermined portion using the segment training data set associated with each predetermined portion. 21. A model management computing system configured to perform actions of a method as recited in any one of claims 13 to 20. 22. A non-transitory computer-readable medium having computer-executable instructions stored thereon that, in response to execution by one or more processors of a model management computing system, cause the model management computing system to perform actions of a method as recited in any one of claims 13 to 20. 23. A method of transmitting information from an edge computing device to a model management computing system via a low-bandwidth connection, the method comprising: receiving, by the edge computing device, a high-bandwidth video stream at a first bandwidth in a variable-bandwidth codec from a camera; downsampling, by the edge computing device, the high-bandwidth video stream to a second bandwidth; transcoding, by the edge computing device, the downsampled high-bandwidth video stream to a bandwidth-capped codec to create a low-bandwidth video stream; and transmitting, by the edge computing device, the low-bandwidth video stream to the model management computing system. 24. The method of claim 23, wherein transmitting the low-bandwidth video stream to the model management computing system includes: combining, by the edge computing device, the low-bandwidth video stream with at least one other low-bandwidth video stream to create a combined data stream; and transmitting, by the edge computing device, the combined data stream to the model management computing system via a low-bandwidth connection. 25. The method of claim 24, wherein transmitting the low-bandwidth video stream to the model management computing system further includes: receiving, by the edge computing device, an auxiliary data stream from an equipment device; and adding, by the edge computing device, the auxiliary data stream to the combined data stream. 26. An edge computing device configured to perform actions of a method as recited in any one of claims 23 to 25. 37 27. A non-transitory computer-readable medium having computer-executable instructions stored thereon that, in response to execution by one or more processors of an edge computing device, cause the edge computing device to perform actions of a method as recited in any one of claims 23 to 25. 38
